name: Detroit
blurb: Detroit's sewer infrastructure is among the oldest in Michigan, with many lateral lines dating back 80 to 100 years. Clay pipe failures, root intrusion through cracked joints, and storm drain blockages caused by decades of sediment buildup are everyday realities for Detroit homeowners and commercial property managers.
extra: Detroit Sewer Co. has worked extensively throughout Detroit neighborhoods from Southwest to East side, clearing severe backups and cleaning storm lines that serve large warehouse and retail complexes.
localContext: Detroit's residential neighborhoods range from historic Mexicantown and Corktown to East English Village and Indian Village, each with aging sewer infrastructure that varies by decade of construction. Properties with extended lateral runs across large lots require jetting equipment reaching beyond 200 feet without losing pressure. Commercial corridors along Jefferson, Gratiot, and Michigan Avenue generate heavy runoff that frequently overwhelms storm drain inlets.
commonIssues: The most common sewer problems we resolve in Detroit are root-packed clay lateral lines, collapsed pipe sections in older areas of the city's grid, and storm drains choked with decades of accumulated sediment. We also frequently provide documentation when client laterals connect to city mains that are contributing to a backup, supporting service requests through the Detroit Water and Sewerage Department.

name: Dearborn
blurb: Dearborn's dense residential neighborhoods and industrial corridors mean sewer lines here handle heavy loads year-round. Older clay and cast-iron laterals are common throughout East and West Dearborn, making root removal and hydro jetting essential preventive services for property owners.
extra: From commercial properties near the Ford River Rouge Complex to residential streets lined with mature oaks, our team understands the unique sewer challenges Dearborn properties face.
localContext: East Dearborn's dense bungalow neighborhoods and the commercial strips along Michigan Avenue are home to some of the most frequently serviced sewer lines in Wayne County. West Dearborn's mix of mid-century ranch homes and newer commercial development requires both root removal in established residential laterals and storm drain service for large surface parking areas. The city's active restaurant scene along Schaefer and Warren adds significant grease load to commercial sewer mains.
commonIssues: Grease buildup is the primary commercial sewer problem we encounter in Dearborn, particularly in the restaurant corridors that run through both East and West Dearborn. Residential clients most often call us for root removal and hydro jetting after repeated backup events that conventional cable snaking failed to permanently resolve. Clay lateral lines in Dearborn's older neighborhoods often show significant root bridging that requires multiple jetting passes to fully clear.

name: Southfield
blurb: Southfield is home to hundreds of office towers, apartment complexes, and retail centers, all of which depend on well-maintained sewer and storm drain systems. Grease accumulation in commercial lines and root intrusion in aging residential laterals are the most common issues we address here.
extra: Our high-volume hydro jetting equipment is especially well-suited for the large commercial sewer mains found throughout Southfield's busy I-696 and Northwestern Highway corridors.
localContext: Southfield's office tower district along the Lodge Freeway and Northwestern Highway corridor hosts hundreds of commercial tenants who depend on properly maintained sanitary sewer systems and storm drains. Southfield's residential sections south of 10 Mile feature mid-century homes with original clay lateral pipes, which are particularly vulnerable to root intrusion and joint separation as they enter their sixth and seventh decades of service.
commonIssues: Commercial sewer main blockages caused by grease from building food service operations and accumulation of debris in storm drains are the most frequent issues we handle in Southfield. In residential areas, root intrusion from the city's established tree canopy is a persistent problem that benefits from annual or biennial hydro jetting. Apartment complex sewer mains also require periodic servicing as building population and grease load exceed what occasional snaking can manage.

name: Livonia
blurb: Much of Livonia was developed in the 1950s and 1960s, and the sewer laterals installed during that era are reaching the end of their service life. Tree-lined streets contribute to aggressive root intrusion, and slow drains or recurring backups in Livonia homes are often a sign that professional attention is overdue.
extra: Detroit Sewer Co. serves homeowners and businesses throughout Livonia, restoring flow quickly and providing camera inspections that reveal exactly what is happening inside the pipe before costly assumptions are made.
localContext: Livonia developed intensively through the 1950s and 1960s, which means a large portion of the city's residential sewer laterals are vitrified clay pipe now more than 60 years old. These pipes are at or past their design service life, and they experience accelerating root intrusion as trees seek out nutrients through cracked or separated joints. Livonia's commercial corridors along Plymouth Road, Five Mile, and Seven Mile Road also generate food service grease that requires periodic clearing.
commonIssues: The most common call from Livonia homeowners is a basement backup caused by roots that have grown into the sewer lateral over years without cleaning or inspection. Camera inspection in Livonia frequently reveals pipes that are partially or fully root-bridged, with some sections showing joint separation requiring follow-up assessment. Homeowners who have experienced two or more backups within a few years typically find that a full camera inspection and targeted root removal resolves the recurring cycle.

name: Warren
blurb: Warren is Macomb County's largest city and carries one of Southeast Michigan's heaviest mixes of residential and industrial sewer demand. Older neighborhoods in South Warren frequently experience root intrusion and collapsing clay laterals, while commercial properties along Van Dyke and Groesbeck need periodic storm drain service to handle heavy runoff.
extra: Our crews are familiar with Warren's sewer grid and work efficiently to clear blockages, remove roots, and document conditions that may require municipal follow-up.
localContext: Warren's residential areas are concentrated in the southern portion of the city, where older mid-century housing stock and large tree-lined streets create classic conditions for clay pipe root intrusion. The industrial and commercial zones along Van Dyke, Mound Road, and Groesbeck Avenue require periodic storm drain maintenance to keep high-volume runoff from backing up into parking lots and loading areas. Warren's large footprint means lateral line conditions vary significantly between its older south end and the more recently developed northern sections.
commonIssues: Commercial storm drain blockages and residential root intrusion are the two most common sewer problems we address in Warren. South Warren residential jobs frequently involve laterals that have never been professionally cleaned, requiring multiple jetting passes to clear years of accumulated root growth and debris. We also perform camera inspections for Warren homeowners preparing to sell, where undisclosed sewer condition can become a significant issue in real estate transactions.

name: Royal Oak
blurb: Royal Oak's vibrant downtown restaurant scene generates significant grease load in its commercial sewer mains, while the city's older bungalow neighborhoods contend with clay pipe laterals increasingly compromised by tree roots. Both issues are well within our capabilities.
extra: We serve restaurants, apartment buildings, and single-family homes throughout Royal Oak, keeping their sewer systems compliant and flowing with scheduled hydro jetting and emergency response.
localContext: Downtown Royal Oak's restaurant and retail district generates significant grease and food waste that accumulates in the commercial sewer mains, particularly along Main Street and Washington Avenue. Surrounding neighborhoods like Vinsetta Park and Oakridge feature homes from the 1920s through 1950s with original clay lateral pipes that benefit from professional root removal. Royal Oak's mature oak and maple canopy is particularly aggressive at finding entry points in aging pipes.
commonIssues: Recurring kitchen drain backups are the most common commercial sewer call we receive from Royal Oak's busy food service businesses, and hydro jetting is the only method that reliably restores full capacity in grease-coated commercial mains. In residential areas, our most frequent jobs involve clearing clay lateral lines that have become partially or fully blocked with roots, often following several failed attempts with cable equipment that cut a hole through the root mass without removing it.

name: Troy
blurb: Troy's rapid commercial expansion along the Big Beaver and Crooks Road corridors has created significant stormwater management demands, and older residential sections near Maple Road face the same root intrusion issues common to Oakland County's mid-century neighborhoods.
extra: Detroit Sewer Co. handles Troy's sewer and storm drain needs with the same professional approach we bring to every job, whether it's a single-family home backup or a multi-acre commercial storm drain system.
localContext: The older residential areas of Troy south of Big Beaver Road were developed through the 1960s and 1970s, and their sewer laterals are aging into the range where professional attention becomes essential for preventing emergency backups. Troy's commercial corridors around the Somerset Collection and along Rochester Road include large parking lots whose storm drains accumulate significant sediment and debris between cleanings. Mixed residential and commercial zoning in parts of Troy creates diverse service demands across relatively short distances.
commonIssues: Root intrusion in older Troy neighborhoods and storm drain accumulation in commercial areas are the two primary issues we address most frequently. Camera inspections in Troy residential areas also reveal belly sections, where pipe has settled and created a low point that collects debris and requires more frequent maintenance than a properly graded line. Troy's commercial property managers benefit from scheduled annual storm drain cleaning, which prevents the standing water and flooding complaints that accumulate when inlets go unserviced through heavy use seasons.

name: Ferndale
blurb: Ferndale is a compact, densely developed suburb where older sewer infrastructure is the norm and mature street trees mean root intrusion is a persistent problem. Many Ferndale homes still have the original terra-cotta lateral lines, and a single storm event can trigger backups across entire blocks.
extra: Our targeted root removal and hydro jetting services are particularly effective in Ferndale's close-set infrastructure, where precision matters as much as power.
localContext: Ferndale is one of Oakland County's most densely built communities, with bungalows and small lots from the 1920s through 1950s sitting directly above some of the oldest sewer lateral pipe in the county. The commercial section along Woodward Avenue adds grease management responsibility on top of the root intrusion challenges in the residential grid. Ferndale's ongoing neighborhood investment and rising property values make proactive sewer maintenance an increasingly important part of property ownership here.
commonIssues: Root intrusion into clay lateral lines is nearly universal in Ferndale's residential areas, and the close spacing of properties means root problems in one lateral often indicate similar conditions in neighboring homes. We frequently work in Ferndale to clear root masses that have caused recurring backups for years before the homeowner sought a specialist. After root clearing, we recommend camera inspection in Ferndale to check pipe wall condition, as the age of many Ferndale laterals means joint separation and partial collapse are common findings alongside root intrusion.

name: Farmington Hills
blurb: Farmington Hills spans a wide range of property ages, from 1970s subdivisions to newer commercial developments, and sewer issues here range from root-choked residential laterals to storm drain systems struggling to handle intense rainfall. Preventive maintenance pays dividends in a city where both residential and commercial property values are high.
extra: Detroit Sewer Co. provides hydro jetting, root removal, and camera inspection services throughout Farmington Hills, helping property owners stay ahead of costly sewer failures.
localContext: Farmington Hills spans a wide development timeline, from older sections near Grand River Avenue built in the 1960s to newer subdivisions near 12 Mile and Haggerty Road developed in the 1990s and 2000s. This variation in property age means sewer issues range from clay pipe root intrusion in older sections to construction defects in newer laterals best identified through camera inspection. Farmington Hills' commercial zones along Northwestern Highway and Orchard Lake Road include both restaurant operations and office buildings, each with distinct sewer maintenance needs.
commonIssues: Older Farmington Hills neighborhoods most frequently need root removal and hydro jetting, while newer sections more often present with settlement-related belly sections or construction defects that camera inspection can locate precisely. Commercial and office properties in Farmington Hills also need periodic storm drain cleaning, particularly after heavy fall leaf accumulation clogs inlet grates and reduces drainage capacity. Farmington Hills homeowners purchasing older properties are increasingly requesting sewer camera inspections as part of due diligence before closing.

name: Ann Arbor
blurb: Ann Arbor's combination of 19th-century downtown infrastructure, mid-century residential neighborhoods, and modern University of Michigan facilities creates a uniquely varied set of sewer challenges. Older lateral lines near campus and in Old West Side frequently need root removal and hydro jetting to stay clear.
extra: We travel regularly to Ann Arbor for residential and commercial clients who value professional sewer service backed by advanced equipment and honest assessments.
localContext: Ann Arbor's Old West Side, Burns Park, and Kerrytown neighborhoods contain some of the oldest residential sewer laterals in Southeast Michigan, many dating to the early 20th century. University of Michigan facilities and dense student housing areas create high-volume sewer demand, while commercial corridors like Main Street and South University have active food service operations that contribute grease to sewer mains. Ann Arbor's commitment to older housing stock means many property owners eventually face the significant lateral maintenance issues that come with century-old pipes.
commonIssues: Root intrusion and deteriorated clay laterals are the most common residential sewer issues in Ann Arbor's older neighborhoods, where the combination of pipe age and large tree canopy creates nearly ideal conditions for root entry. Commercial grease accumulation in downtown sewer mains is a persistent issue for the city's restaurant district. Camera inspections in Ann Arbor frequently identify pipe conditions that have been deteriorating for years before a backup event brings the problem to the surface.

name: Sterling Heights
blurb: As one of Metro Detroit's largest suburbs, Sterling Heights has both extensive residential sewer networks and major commercial corridors along Mound Road and Van Dyke that require periodic storm drain maintenance. Root intrusion and grease buildup are the most frequent issues we resolve for Sterling Heights clients.
extra: Our equipment handles the volume and scale that Sterling Heights sewer systems demand, providing thorough cleaning and detailed inspection reports when conditions warrant.
localContext: Sterling Heights has established residential neighborhoods developed in the 1970s and 1980s alongside an active commercial and light-industrial corridor along Mound Road and Van Dyke Avenue. The residential sections are entering the age range where lateral pipe maintenance becomes important, particularly for homeowners with mature trees whose roots are actively seeking entry points in aging clay and cast-iron pipes. Sterling Heights' large retail and industrial properties generate significant storm drain demand from extensive impervious parking and staging areas.
commonIssues: Hydro jetting for grease accumulation in commercial sewer mains and root removal in aging residential laterals are the most common services we provide in Sterling Heights. Storm drain cleaning for commercial parking lots is also a regular request, particularly for larger retail and industrial properties that experience standing water after heavy rain. Sterling Heights residential clients who call us after repeated cable snaking attempts typically find that hydro jetting resolves the problem far more completely and durably.

name: Canton
blurb: Canton Township has grown dramatically over the past two decades, and with that growth comes increasing demand on both municipal sewer capacity and private lateral lines. Newer construction does not mean trouble-free sewer lines; improper installation and settlement can cause early failures that video camera inspection quickly identifies.
extra: Detroit Sewer Co. serves Canton homeowners and commercial property managers with fast response, professional equipment, and clear explanations of what we find and what it means for your property.
localContext: Canton Township's rapid growth means it has a diverse mix of property vintages, from newly constructed subdivisions where installation quality issues may not yet have caused a backup to 1990s-era neighborhoods whose sewer systems are beginning to show their age. The township's commercial corridors along Ford Road and Cherry Hill generate both food service grease and significant storm drain demand from large parking areas serving retail and restaurant uses. Canton's newer sewer infrastructure still requires professional maintenance as it enters its second and third decade of service.
commonIssues: Camera inspections are particularly valuable in Canton because many homeowners have never had their sewer laterals professionally evaluated. We frequently identify root intrusion, improper slope, and settlement-related belly sections in Canton properties before they reach the backup stage, saving homeowners from emergency service calls that are far more disruptive and expensive. Commercial grease accumulation and storm drain blockages are regular calls from Canton's retail and restaurant corridors along Ford Road.

name: Novi
blurb: Novi's booming commercial development along I-96 and Twelve Mile Road has created significant stormwater management responsibilities for shopping centers, office parks, and mixed-use developments. Residential areas in Novi also face lateral line issues as the community's original sewer infrastructure ages.
extra: We provide scheduled storm drain cleaning and emergency sewer service throughout Novi, keeping commercial properties compliant and residential sewer systems running reliably.
localContext: Novi's commercial development along I-96, Twelve Mile Road, and Grand River Avenue includes major retail centers, office parks, and restaurant clusters whose sewer and storm drain systems require professional maintenance to function properly during heavy rain events. Residential sections of Novi range from established neighborhoods near Novi Road to newer subdivisions where the infrastructure is still relatively young but not immune to installation-related defects. Novi's active growth continues to add commercial properties whose storm drain systems need servicing from the first year of operation.
commonIssues: Storm drain maintenance is the most frequent commercial service we provide in Novi, where large impervious parking surfaces generate significant runoff that requires properly functioning inlets and drains to manage without flooding. Residential calls in Novi are more often hydro jetting and camera inspection than emergency backups, since newer infrastructure generally performs better than the aging clay systems common in older Metro Detroit communities. We also serve Novi homeowners purchasing resale properties who want a camera inspection before closing on a home with an unknown sewer history.

name: Pontiac
blurb: Pontiac is an older industrial city with a sewer network that reflects its history: aging cast-iron and clay mains, heavy root intrusion in residential areas, and storm drains that frequently need clearing after heavy rain. We approach Pontiac jobs with the heavy-duty equipment these conditions demand.
extra: Detroit Sewer Co. has experience navigating Pontiac's older infrastructure, including coordination with the city when blockages originate in municipal mains rather than private laterals.
localContext: Pontiac's historic neighborhoods around the waterways and downtown core contain some of the most aged and infrastructure-stressed sewer networks we work with in Oakland County. Post-industrial economic challenges have deferred maintenance on both public mains and private laterals in many areas, and the combination of old pipe and heavy tree canopy means root intrusion is extensive throughout much of the city's residential grid. Pontiac's commercial properties often have storm drains that have received little professional attention in many years.
commonIssues: Root removal and emergency sewer backup clearing are the most common calls we receive from Pontiac property owners. Lateral lines in Pontiac frequently contain significant root intrusion compounded by partial pipe collapse, and we regularly follow up clearing with camera inspection to document conditions that may warrant coordination with the city about main line involvement. Pontiac is one of the markets where our ability to produce written condition reports becomes particularly valuable for property owners dealing with municipal infrastructure issues.

name: Saginaw
blurb: Saginaw's aging urban infrastructure and industrial legacy create demanding sewer conditions: clay lateral failures, root intrusion, and storm drain systems stressed by aging combined sewer overflows. Property owners here benefit from professional hydro jetting and camera inspection to keep their systems functioning between municipal upgrades.
extra: Detroit Sewer Co. extends service to Saginaw for clients who need the capabilities of Metro Detroit's most experienced sewer specialists brought to their Mid-Michigan properties.
localContext: Saginaw's mid-Michigan location puts it at a geographic distance from Metro Detroit, but the quality of sewer service available locally does not always match what larger urban markets provide. Detroit Sewer Co. travels to Saginaw for clients who need heavy-duty hydro jetting, root removal with specialized cutting heads, or thorough video inspection that local providers cannot perform. Saginaw's aging housing stock and legacy industrial districts both present challenging sewer conditions that require the right equipment to address effectively.
commonIssues: Aging combined sewer overflow infrastructure, clay lateral deterioration, and storm drain blockages from industrial-area accumulation are the primary sewer challenges in Saginaw. Our Warthog root removal system and high-volume jetting equipment are particularly effective on the older pipe found throughout Saginaw's residential and commercial districts. Clients in Saginaw who call us typically do so after exhausting local options and needing a higher level of capability and equipment than what is readily available in the market.

name: Port Huron
blurb: Port Huron's waterfront location and older downtown sewer infrastructure mean that root intrusion, sediment accumulation, and storm drain backups are common concerns for property owners. Seasonal freezing and thawing accelerates deterioration of older clay pipe laterals throughout the city.
extra: Detroit Sewer Co. serves Port Huron and St. Clair County clients seeking professional sewer cleaning, root removal, and camera inspection backed by top-tier equipment and transparent service.
localContext: Port Huron's position at the confluence of Lake Huron and the St. Clair River creates a unique environment for sewer infrastructure, where seasonal freeze-thaw cycles accelerate clay pipe deterioration and waterfront properties face elevated groundwater that stresses aging lateral lines year-round. The downtown historic district and waterfront commercial areas include restaurant and tourism operations whose sewer and storm drain systems see heavy seasonal demand during the warm months when Port Huron draws the most visitors.
commonIssues: Clay pipe failure and root intrusion are the dominant residential sewer problems in Port Huron, with the freeze-thaw cycle playing a larger role in joint separation here than in more sheltered communities closer to Detroit. Commercial clients most frequently need storm drain clearing and grease removal, particularly in the tourist-season-heavy waterfront food service corridor. We travel to Port Huron for clients who want the capabilities that come with Metro Detroit's most experienced sewer team and heavy-duty equipment.
